prompt: Create three simple, clear diagrams labelled A, B, and C side by side in a row. A: A vertical thermometer from -10 to 50 degrees with labelled tick marks every 10 degrees. The red liquid level is at 22 degrees. B: A horizontal ruler from 0 to 15 cm with millimetre ticks. A red marker line is at 6.3 cm. C: A transparent measuring jug marked every 100 mL from 0 to 1000 mL. Blue water level at 600 mL. Use clean black lines, white background, clear labels.

Create three simple, clear diagrams labelled A, B, and C side by side. A: A dial-type kitchen scale from 0 to 500 g. Needle pointing at 250 g. B: A speedometer dial from 0 to 200 km/h. Needle at 60 km/h. C: A protractor semicircle 0 to 180 degrees with a line at 45 degrees. Use clean black lines, white background, clear labels.

Create three simple, clear diagrams labelled A, B, and C side by side. A: A bathroom scale dial from 0 to 100 kg. Needle at 55 kg. B: A barometer from 960 to 1050 hPa. Needle at 1013 hPa. C: A measuring tape from 0 to 100 cm, marked every 10 cm. Arrow at 23 cm. Use clean black lines, white background, clear labels.

Create three simple, clear diagrams labelled A, B, and C side by side. A: A vertical thermometer -10 to 40 degrees with ticks every 5 degrees. Liquid at approximately 18 degrees (between 15 and 20). B: A horizontal ruler 0 to 20 cm with mm ticks. Arrow at 67 mm (6.7 cm). C: A graduated cylinder marked every 5 mL from 0 to 100 mL. Water level at 37 mL. Use clean black lines, white background, clear labels.

Create three simple, clear diagrams labelled A, B, and C side by side. A: A circular dial 0 to 300 psi. Needle at 175 psi. B: A barometer dial 950 to 1050 hPa. Needle at 1005 hPa. C: A car speedometer 0 to 200 km/h with marks every 20. Needle at 140 km/h. Use clean black lines, white background, clear labels.

Create three simple, clear diagrams labelled A, B, and C side by side. A: A flow meter 0 to 100 L/min with marks every 10. Needle at 85 L/min. B: A spring scale 0 to 5 N with marks every 0.5 N. Needle at 3.5 N. C: A measuring jug with marks every 50 mL from 0 to 1000 mL. Water at 850 mL. Use clean black lines, white background, clear labels.

Create three simple, clear diagrams labelled A, B, and C side by side. A: A vertical thermometer -10 to 50, liquid just above the 15 mark at about 16 degrees. B: A ruler 0 to 10 cm with a smudge between 4 and 5 cm. Arrow heart at about 4.3 cm. C: A measuring jug 0 to 500 mL with marks every 20 mL. Meniscus at about 380 mL. Use clean black lines, white background, clear labels.

Create three simple, clear diagrams labelled A, B, and C side by side. A: A dial scale 0 to 500 with marks every 25. Needle pointing between 200 and 250 at about 220. B: A speedometer 0 to 120 km/h with small marks. Needle at about 68 km/h. C: A 180-degree protractor. A line creates an angle of about 73 degrees (past 70, before 75). Use clean black lines, white background, clear labels.

Create three simple, clear diagrams labelled A, B, and C side by side. A: A barometer 960-1050 with needle at about 1008 hPa. B: A spring scale 0 to 3 kg with marks every 0.25 kg. Needle at about 1.2 kg. C: A flow meter 0 to 200 L/min with marks. Needle at about 160 L/min. Use clean black lines, white background, clear labels.

Three simple diagrams labelled A, B, C side by side. A: A simple floor plan of a rectangular room 8 cm by 5 cm with a scale bar '1 cm = 1 m'. B: A line drawing of a car 6 cm long with label 'Scale 1:50'. C: A map with two towns 4 cm apart and a scale bar '0 1 2 3 4 5 km'. Clean black lines, white background.

Three simple diagrams labelled A, B, C side by side. A: A house plan showing a 3 cm window labelled 'Scale 1:100'. B: A model plane 40 cm long labelled 'Scale 1:25'. C: A blueprint line measuring 4 cm labelled '1:50'. Clean black lines, white background.

Three simple diagrams labelled A, B, C side by side. A: A map scale bar '1 cm = 5 km' with towns 3 cm apart. B: A 5 cm coin drawing labelled 'Scale 2:1'. C: A floor plan 5 cm wide labelled 'Scale 1:50'. Clean black lines, white background.

Three simple diagrams labelled A, B, C side by side. A: A map with 4.6 cm line between points, scale bar '1:25000' (1 cm = 0.25 km). B: A car design drawing 45 cm long labelled 'Scale 1:10'. C: A model railway 25 cm long labelled 'Scale 1:87'. Clean black lines, white background.

Three simple diagrams labelled A, B, C side by side. A: An architect plan with 1.2 cm window, scale '1:200'. B: A satellite image of a 3.2 cm x 2.8 cm field, scale '1:50000'. C: A map with 12.5 cm distance, scale '1:100000'. Clean black lines, white background.

Three simple diagrams labelled A, B, C side by side. A: A scale drawing of a boat 30 cm long labelled 'Scale 1:40'. B: A floor plan room 4.8 cm x 3.6 cm labelled '1:100'. C: A photo of a bug 6 cm long labelled '10x magnification'. Clean black lines, white background.

Three simple diagrams labelled A, B, C side by side. A: A map cross-section with a 4.6 cm rise, scale '1 cm = 50 m vertical'. B: A park plan with a 0.8 cm wide path, scale '1:500'. C: A photo of an 8 cm tall building labelled 'Scale 1:400'. Clean black lines, white background.

Three simple diagrams labelled A, B, C side by side. A: A map of a lake 5.5 cm across, scale '1:10000'. B: A house model 15 cm high labelled 'Scale 1:50'. C: A cell diagram 3 cm across labelled 'Microscope view'. Clean black lines, white background.

Three simple diagrams labelled A, B, C side by side. A: A wall plan 7.2 cm long labelled 'Scale 1:50'. B: A tree 2 cm wide on a garden plan labelled 'Scale 1:300'. C: An aerial photo with 0.5 cm building, scale '1:5000'. Clean black lines, white background.
